title: Chennamangalam Temple
tagline: Chennamangalam Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Chennamangalam Temple is situated in Chorode Panchayat of Vatakara Taluka in Kozhikode District of Kerala, India.The main Pratishtha is Lord Ayyappa.
overview.description: Chennamangalam Temple is situated in Chorode Panchayat of Vatakara Taluka in Kozhikode District of Kerala, India.The main Pratishtha is Lord Ayyappa. However Lords Shiva, Ganesh, Vishnu and Annapurneshwari are also worshipped in this temple. This is considered as one of oldest temples in Kerala, and also one of the very few temples which has Dhwaja Prathishta. This is situated 2 km from town and is near to Chorode.

topPlaces.name: Chorode
topPlaces.description: Chorode is a village in Kozhikode district in the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Madappally, Vatakara
topPlaces.description: Madappally is a small town near Vatakara in Kozhikode District, Kerala, India. The 'Pally' name denotes Buddhism.

topPlaces.name: Onchiam
topPlaces.description: Onchiyam is a village in Kozhikode district in the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Karthikappalli
topPlaces.description: Karthikappalli is a small village in Kozhikode district in the state of Kerala, India. The population was about 19,021 people according to the 2011 Census of India.

topPlaces.name: Lokanarkavu Temple
topPlaces.description: Lokanarkavu Temple is an ancient Hindu temple dedicated to goddess Durga devi situated in Memunda, 4 km from Vatakara, in Kozhikode District, North Malabar region of Kerala state of south India. Lokanarkavu is a short form of Lokamalayarkavu which means lokam (world) made of mala (mountain), aaru (river) and kavu (grove).

topPlaces.name: Edacheri
topPlaces.description: Edacheri is a small panchayath in the district of Kozhikode in the South Indian state of Kerala.

topPlaces.name: Villiappally
topPlaces.description: Villiappally is a census town in Kozhikode district in the Indian state of Kerala. Villiappally is the headquarters of Villiappally Panchayath, which contains two villages, Memunda and Villiappally.

topPlaces.name: Memunda
topPlaces.description: Memunda is a village near Vatakara town in Kozhikode district of Kerala, south India. The Lokanarkavu Temple is located at Memunda.

Lokanarkavu Temple is an ancient Hindu temple dedicated to goddess Durga devi situated in Memunda, 4 km from Vatakara, in Kozhikode District, North Malabar region of Kerala state of south India. Lokanarkavu is a short form of Lokamalayarkavu which means lokam (world) made of mala (mountain), aaru (river) and kavu (grove).

Villiappally is a census town in Kozhikode district in the Indian state of Kerala. Villiappally is the headquarters of Villiappally Panchayath, which contains two villages, Memunda and Villiappally.
Memunda is a village near Vatakara town in Kozhikode district of Kerala, south India. The Lokanarkavu Temple is located at Memunda.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Chennamangalam Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Chennamangalam Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Chennamangalam Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Calicut International Airport, Kozhikode (67 km) and the nearest railway station is Mysuru Junction (138 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Chennamangalam Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Chennamangalam Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Chennamangalam Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Chennamangalam Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Chennamangalam Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Chorode, Madappally, Vatakara, Onchiam, Karthikappalli, Lokanarkavu Temple.
